title: docent film spec
description: The contract between the agent layer (which authors a spec) and the engine (which renders it). Versioned; the engine validates every spec against the structural subset of this in cli/validate.ts.

metrics.description: quantities scenes — figure cards whose number is a tweened value that counts up across beats

nodes.description: structure scenes — the component boxes of the diagram

xAxis.description: chart scenes — the horizontal axis domain

yAxis.description: chart scenes — the vertical axis domain

series.description: chart scenes — the plotted series (line / bars / point)

text.description: passage scenes — the plain-text artifact (a poem, prose, a primary source), typeset as prose/verse with line breaks preserved

marks.description: passage scenes — the annotatable spans of `text`, activated by beats' reveal/focus

image.description: figure scenes — the still image, resolved via staticFile (a bare filename resolves under public/figures/)

callouts.description: figure scenes — labelled markers pinned to normalized regions of `image`, activated by beats' reveal/focus

id.description: unique across the whole film — the TTS cache keys on it

set.description: tween directive — named values this beat drives; the engine eases each from the value the prior set-beat held

transform.description: morph directive — re-binds named nodes to new definitions; the engine eases old→new across this beat (cross-beat object identity)

node.description: a component box in a structure diagram. `as` is its representation (default `box`, the labelled Card); a `transform` can morph one node into another representation.

as.description: the node's representation — `box` is the Card; matrix/vector/grid draw `cells`; `code` a code window

transform.description: one morph — re-binds a node to a new definition. `node` names an existing node id in the scene; `into` is a partial Node whose fields override the node's prior definition.

node.description: the id of an existing node in this scene

into.description: a partial Node — only the fields it names change

node-partial.description: a partial Node — every field optional; merged onto the node's prior definition

tween.description: a value the engine interpolates across a beat boundary

to.description: the target the beat drives toward

from.description: explicit start value, used only by the first beat to set this key

metric.description: a figure card whose displayed number IS a tweened value

bind.description: the `set` key this metric's number reads from

axis.description: a chart axis — a labelled domain the engine maps onto STAGE pixels

ticks.description: tick marks along the axis; capped at 10 for legibility

series.description: a plotted chart series. `kind` picks the geometry; a `line` names a `fn` from the closed allowlist or gives explicit `points`, `bars` carries `data`, `point` is a marker bound to a curve.

fn.description: line series — a named function from the closed allowlist; never an arbitrary expression

points.description: line series — an explicit polyline, an alternative to `fn`

data.description: bars series — one datum per bar; capped at 8 for legibility

bind.description: point series — a `set` key giving the marker's x

along.description: point series — the line series id whose curve gives the marker's y

mark.description: passage scenes — one annotatable span. `quote` is the exact substring of the scene's `text` to highlight; the engine underlines it and pins `note` beside it. A beat activates marks by id through reveal/focus.

id.description: unique within the scene; beats reveal/focus this id

quote.description: the exact substring of `text` to locate and highlight

note.description: the short annotation pinned to the span

callout.description: figure scenes — one labelled marker pinned to a region of the image. `at` is a normalized 0..1 (x, y) position. A beat activates callouts by id through reveal/focus.

id.description: unique within the scene; beats reveal/focus this id

at.description: normalized [x, y] position over the image, each in 0..1

note.description: an optional longer note under the label
